description: TLS session ticket cache + rustls session resumption

cold_connect.description: First connection to server (full TLS handshake + PQ KEX)
cold_connect.includes: QUIC handshake + TLS 1.3 with X25519MLKEM768 + ALPN negotiation

warm_connect.description: Second connection to same server (TLS session resumption)
warm_connect.note: Includes first connection setup in each iteration (fresh server per iter). Actual warm connect time is masked by first connection overhead.

pooled_connect.description: Open new bidirectional stream on existing connection
pooled_connect.note: No TLS handshake at all — just QUIC stream creation. Baseline for Track I5 (connection pool).

analysis.cold_connect_breakdown: 240µs = QUIC handshake + TLS 1.3 with X25519MLKEM768 PQ KEX + ALPN
analysis.pooled_vs_cold: 14µs vs 240µs = 17x faster (stream open vs full handshake)
analysis.resumption_benefit: TLS session resumption skips the PQ KEX on reconnect, reducing handshake time. The exact savings depend on whether the server accepts the ticket.
analysis.pooled_benefit: Connection pooling (Track I5) eliminates the handshake entirely — 17x improvement for repeated RPCs to the same peer.

implementation.server_change: send_tls13_tickets = 4 (was 2 default) — more tickets for more resumptions
implementation.client_change: Cached ClientConfig with shared SessionCache reused across dial() calls
implementation.backward_compat: QuicTransport::new() unchanged — no resumption, builds fresh config per dial
implementation.security: 0-RTT early data NOT enabled (replay risk). AAFP handshake still runs after TLS resumption.

verify_criteria.resumption_enabled: PASS — ClientConfig uses Resumption::store() with shared cache
verify_criteria.backward_compat: PASS — new() still works without resumption
